intro: Triplochlamys selloi, also known as Sello's herb or Triplochlamys, is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Brazil, to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Triplochlamys selloi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Triplochlamys selloi include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, fever, and cough.
